title: Engineering Mathematics_ Probability - Expectation - Theoretical/Not Answered

questions.question:
Assume that you are flipping a fair coin, i.e. probability of heads or tails is equal. Then the expected number of coin flips required to obtain two consecutive heads for the first time is.

  1. \( 4 \)
  2. \( 3 \)
  3. \( 6 \)
  4. \( 10 \)
  5. \( 5 \)


TIFR CSE 2011 | Part A | Question: 6

A fair dice (with faces numbered \( 1, . . . , 6 \)) is independently rolled repeatedly. Let \( X \) denote the number of rolls till an even number is seen and let \( Y \) denote the number of rolls till \( 3 \) is seen. Evaluate \( E(Y |X = 2) \).



TIFR CSE 2014 | Part A | Question: 17
